name: Ricotta Pancakes With Gooseberry Relish
intro: batter over the fruit. Use an offset spatula to spread it evenly, forming a layer

ingredients.item: lb/750 g gooseberries or pitted plums, fresh or frozen
orange, rind shaved in long strips
1¼-inch/3-cm piece fresh ginger (about ⅓ oz/10 g), peeled and cut into thin matchsticks
sunflower oil scant ½ cup/100 g crème fraîche salt

method: Place the gooseberries, ¾ cup plus 2½ tablespoons/180 g of the sugar, the orange rind, ginger, and cinnamon in a saucepan and cook over medium-low heat for roughly 30 minutes, until a semithick compote consistency has formed. Set aside to cool.

In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs and ricotta until smooth. Add the flours, the remaining 4½ tablespoons sugar, the vanilla, and ⅛ t easpoon salt and mix well. Cover and refrigerate for 1 hour.

To cook, put a large sauté pan over medium heat, add 1 teaspoon of the oil and, once hot, turn down the heat to low. Make pancakes 4-inches/10-cm in diameter by ⅜-inch/1-cm thick and cook for 3 to 4 minutes on one side before flipping and cooking for a final minute, until golden brown on both sides. It is important the pancakes are cooked over low heat so they start to set on top before being flipped over, or you will end up with splashes of batter all over the pan. Transfer to a plate lined with paper towels and keep somewhere warm while you repeat with the remaining batter, adding oil as you go.
Serve 2 warm pancakes on each plate, with the relish and crème fraîche spooned alongside.
